title: agalinis maritima var. maritima
intro: Agalinis maritima var. maritima, also known as coastal agalinis, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the family Orobanchaceae. It is a parasitic plant that obtains its nutrients by attaching to the roots of other plants, often those in the family Scrophulariaceae. The plant is used in traditional medicine for its purported medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral effects. Coastal agalinis has been used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions to promote digestion, relieve respiratory issues, and boost the immune system.
